summary: sendgrid-sdk typosquats @sendgrid and exfiltrates host/CI metadata at postinstall
details: Unscoped npm package 'sendgrid-sdk' impersonates the official @sendgrid/* SDK scope (@sendgrid/mail, @sendgrid/client). On install, postinstall.js collects installer-side identifiers — OS hostname, FQDN via DNS lookup, Active Directory domain, current working directory basename, Node.js version, configured npm registry, and CI repository identifiers from a curated list of CI environment variables (GITHUB_REPOSITORY, CIRCLE_*, GITLAB CI_PROJECT_PATH, BITBUCKET_REPO_FULL_NAME, Azure BUILD_REPOSITORY_URI, TRAVIS_REPO_SLUG, JENKINS_URL, CI_SERVER_URL) — and transmits them as query parameters to a hardcoded bare IP over plain HTTP (http://46.224.67.169:3000/ping). The README labels the package a 'security research honeypot,' but the behavior fires automatically on `npm install` and leaks internal CI topology, AD domain names, and private registry URLs to an attacker-controlled endpoint regardless of the disclosure. Anyone installing this by typo from the legitimate SendGrid scope is harmed.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
